title: Your first session
prompt: Your first session — a gentle welcome into the practice.
script:
Welcome. <break time="3s" /> Take your seat, and let your body settle into whatever shape feels easy right now. <break time="3s" /> You don't need to fix your posture, or clear your mind, or do anything special — you're already here, and that's enough. <break time="6s" /> In this practice, we'll use your breath as an anchor. <break time="3s" /> What that means is simple: when your mind wanders, and it will, you'll gently bring your attention back to one small place — the tip of your nose, where the air passes in and out. <break time="6s" /> That little spot is your home base. <break time="3s" /> Let's try three slow breaths together, just to arrive. <break time="3s" /> Breathe in through the nose… <break time="3s" /> and softly out. <break time="3s" /> Again, in… <break time="3s" /> and out, letting the shoulders drop. <break time="3s" /> One more — in… <break time="3s" /> and a long, easy release. <break time="6s" />

Now let your attention drift gently up to the crown of your head. <break time="3s" /> Notice whatever is there — maybe warmth, maybe tingling, maybe nothing at all. <break time="3s" /> That's okay. Just noticing is the practice.

<break time="6s" />

Let that attention move down across your forehead, softening the space between your eyebrows. <break time="3s" /> And down to the jaw — unclench it slightly, let the teeth part. <break time="3s" /> Imagine a soft, warm light, like sunlight through a window, beginning to spread down into your shoulders.

<break time="6s" />

Let your shoulders drop away from your ears. <break time="3s" /> Feel that warmth move into your chest, rising and falling with the breath. <break time="3s" /> Into the belly, which can be soft here — it doesn't need to be held in.

<break time="6s" />

Down through the hips, the thighs, letting them grow heavy. <break time="3s" /> Past the knees, into the calves, the ankles. <break time="3s" /> And finally all the way to the soles of your feet.

<break time="6s" />

Take a moment to feel the whole body at once — warm, lit from within, a little lighter than before. <break time="6s" /> And return, softly, to the breath at the nostrils. <break time="3s" />

Now let the body rest just as it is. <break time="6s" />

Come back to the breath at the nostrils. <break time="9s" />

Feel that cool thread of air arriving. <break time="6s" />

And the softer warmth as it leaves. <break time="6s" />

Nothing to change here — just noticing. <break time="6s" />

The breath is already happening, all on its own. <break time="6s" />

Let it breathe you. <break time="6s" />

If the mind drifts, that's alright — simply return. <break time="6s" />

Back to this breath, right at the tip of the nose. <break time="6s" />

Resting here, quiet and easy. <break time="6s" />

Still resting with the breath, gentle at the nostrils. <break time="3s" /> And now, a quiet question — what would you like this practice to be for you? <break time="6s" /> No need to search for the right answer. <break time="3s" /> Just let something surface on its own. <break time="6s" /> Maybe a word — rest, or ease, or simply here. <break time="3s" /> Maybe a feeling, without any word at all. <break time="6s" /> Breathe in... and as you breathe out, let that quiet intention settle somewhere inside you. <break time="3s" /> Whatever came is enough. <break time="6s" /> And the breath, still here, still steady, holding it all. <break time="3s" />

Let whatever you named settle quietly into the background. <break time="6s" />

Now, gently deepen the breath. <break time="3s" /> A slow breath in through the nose... and a longer breath out through the mouth. <break time="6s" />

Begin to bring small movement back. <break time="3s" /> Wiggle your fingers. <break time="3s" /> Wiggle your toes. <break time="6s" />

This quiet moment you just gave yourself — this is your practice. <break time="6s" />

And whenever you're ready, softly blink your eyes open, carrying this steadiness with you.
